title: Audience Member
description: A CRM audience member identified by a buyer-assigned external_id and at least one matchable identifier. All identifiers must be normalized before hashing: emails to lowercase+trim, phone numbers to E.164 format (e.g. +12065551234). Providing multiple identifiers for the same person improves match rates. Composite identifiers (e.g. hashed first name + last name + zip for Google Customer Match) are not yet standardized — use the ext field for platform-specific extensions.

external_id.description: Buyer-assigned stable identifier for this audience member (e.g. CRM record ID, loyalty ID). Used for deduplication, removal, and cross-referencing with buyer systems. Adapters for CDPs that don't natively assign IDs can derive one (e.g. hash of the member's identifiers).

hashed_email.description: Hashed email identifier, hex-encoded lowercase, 64 hex chars. The hash scheme is negotiated via the seller's `privacy.audience_hash_schemes` capability (see get_adcp_capabilities). For `hmac_sha256` (required in 4.0, opt-in in 3.x), the value MUST be HMAC-SHA-256(key=per-seller-onboarding-key, msg=normalized_email), where the key is a ≥256-bit CSPRNG secret exchanged out-of-band at onboarding and rotated at least annually. For `sha256_plain` (legacy 3.x default), the value MUST be SHA-256(normalized_email). Email is normalized to lowercase+trim before hashing. NOTE: hashing provides cross-seller-correlation resistance (a given identifier produces a different value per seller when HMAC is used) — it is NOT anonymization. Any party that knows the cleartext identifier and the applicable keying material can reproduce the hash. See docs/building/implementation/security.mdx §PII hashing.

hashed_phone.description: Hashed phone identifier, hex-encoded lowercase, 64 hex chars. The hash scheme is negotiated via the seller's `privacy.audience_hash_schemes` capability (see get_adcp_capabilities). For `hmac_sha256` (required in 4.0, opt-in in 3.x), the value MUST be HMAC-SHA-256(key=per-seller-onboarding-key, msg=normalized_phone), where the key is a ≥256-bit CSPRNG secret exchanged out-of-band at onboarding and rotated at least annually. For `sha256_plain` (legacy 3.x default), the value MUST be SHA-256(normalized_phone). Phone is normalized to E.164 (e.g. +12065551234) before hashing. NOTE: hashing provides cross-seller-correlation resistance (a given identifier produces a different value per seller when HMAC is used) — it is NOT anonymization. Any party that knows the cleartext identifier and the applicable keying material can reproduce the hash. See docs/building/implementation/security.mdx §PII hashing.

uids.description: Universal ID values (MAIDs, RampID, UID2, etc.) for user matching.
